---
title: "W1_DQ"
author: "AmritaPudasaini"
date: "2026-03-15"
output: html_document
---
# Load the churn dataset
```{r}
churn <- read.csv("C:/Users/DELL/Downloads/churn-bigml-20.csv")
```
# View structure of dataset
```{r}
str(churn)
```
# Check missing values
```{r}
colSums(is.na(churn))
```

```{r}
# Check unique states
unique(churn$State)
# Check unique area codes
unique(churn$Area.code)
# Compare state and area code
table(churn$State, churn$Area.code)
```

# Create boxplot to detect outliers
```{r}
boxplot(churn$Customer.service.calls,
        main = "Distribution of Customer Service Calls",
        ylab = "Number of Calls",
        col = "lightblue")
```
# Load adult dataset
```{r}
adult <- read.csv("C:/Users/DELL/Downloads/adlt_data.csv")
adult
```
# Check dataset structure
```{r}
str(adult)
```
# Display first 10 rows
```{r}
head(adult, 10)
```
# Select numeric variables
```{r}
numeric_vars <- adult[, sapply(adult, is.numeric)]
numeric_vars
```

# Compute correlation matrix
```{r}
cor(numeric_vars)
```
# Creating Bar Charts for Categorical Variables with Income Overlay
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Workclass vs Income
ggplot(adult, aes(x = workclass, fill = income)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Income Distribution by Workclass",
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Marital Status vs Income
ggplot(adult, aes(x = marital_status, fill = income)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Income Distribution by Marital Status",
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Occupation vs Income
ggplot(adult, aes(x = occupation, fill = income)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Income Distribution by Occupation",
       y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
